note: Each Topic is a full-stack operational unit evaluated under REAL_EXPERIENCE_CLOSURE_v2 without grandfathering. Source evidence must distinguish present runtime proof from planned requirements.

evidence_contract.rule: A planned path or donor reference never establishes runtime readiness.

DSH-000.purpose: Verify system integrity — all infrastructure, contracts, guards, and toolchain gates must pass before any DSH slice implementation begins.
DSH-000.business_meaning: No implementation ships without a clean foundation. This protects all future slices from building on broken infrastructure.

real_experience_required.system: All gate scripts exit 0. No violations. Docker UP. API smoke 200.

WLT-000.purpose: Establish WLT Go backend, DB migrations, reference-only endpoints, DSH shared API layer, Docker runtime. Prove the financial boundary exists before any DSH slice touches WLT data.
WLT-000.business_meaning: WLT is the single source of financial truth. Its runtime must be proven before any DSH slice can reference payment, settlement, or refund status. This prevents DSH from accidentally owning financial state.
in_scope: WLT Go backend with reference-only endpoints
out_of_scope: WLT mutation APIs (payment, refund, settlement, payout)
WLT control-panel screens beyond skeleton proof

real_experience_required.system: WLT API UP at 58083. Reference endpoints return 200. Migration applied. Seeds inserted.

WLT-000.next_slice_note: WLT-001 mutation APIs (payment, refund, settlement) — NOT_APPROVED_YET. Requires separate approval.

DSH-001.purpose: Full-stack store discovery and management across all 5 surfaces. Customers browse stores, operators govern store visibility, role actors get store context.
DSH-001.business_meaning: Every commerce transaction depends on store discovery. Customers need to find and select a store. Partners need to see their store readiness. Captains need pickup context. Field agents verify store eligibility. Operators govern the store catalog.
in_scope: Store list and store detail backend handlers
useStoreRoleContextController (partner/captain/field state and action context; action contract pending)
StoreManagementScreen + StoreAdminKpiStrip + StoreAdminFilters + StoreAdminTable + StoreDetailAdminPanel + StoreAdminStateView (control-panel)

real_experience_required.app_client: Customer can browse the public store list and see loading, success, empty, contract/error, and service-unavailable states. Screenshots are required for each state.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator governs activation, suspension, visibility, and serviceability with authenticated, audited actions and complete visual states.
real_experience_required.app_partner: Partner manages own-store operating status and store profile readiness without accessing another partner's store.
real_experience_required.app_field: Field agent records assigned-store verification outcomes and evidence status without performing approval outside assignment scope.
real_experience_required.app_captain: Captain reports assigned pickup-point readiness without absorbing order assignment, pickup confirmation, or delivery lifecycle.

evidence_quality_note_v1_closure.mobile_screenshot_coverage: SUCCESS_STATE_ONLY — app-client, app-partner, app-captain, app-field each have 1 success screenshot. Error/loading/empty states are unit-tested in shared controllers but not screenshot-verified.
evidence_quality_note_v1_closure.control_panel_screenshot_coverage: SUCCESS_AND_ERROR — control-panel has success, error, and detail-panel screenshots.
evidence_quality_note_v1_closure.unit_test_state_coverage: 121/121 tests PASS including error, empty, loading, service-unavailable, and API classification tests in shared controllers.
evidence_quality_note_v1_closure.v2_gap: Required surfaces lack mandatory visual states and actionable store-domain contracts. The gap blocks closure under the current standard.

tests_required: Store admin governance action and state tests
Partner/field/captain store-action authorization, idempotency, audit, and state tests
evidence_required: Code structure: shared brain in services/dsh/frontend/shared/store/ (useStoreDiscoveryController, useStoreAdminController, useStoreRoleContextController)
UI-only surfaces: no direct fetch/env/business-logic in any of the 5 surface screens (verified by guard:dsh-001-cross-surface-dependency-map)
API contracts: listDshStores + getDshStore in dsh.openapi.yaml
Generated API client: services/dsh/clients/store-discovery-client.ts present and complete
DB migration: dsh-001_store_discovery.sql applied to runtime DB
Docker runtime health: dsh-api UP at 58080
API smoke: GET /dsh/stores returns real data from DB
Tests: pnpm --dir services/dsh test PASS (covers loading/success/empty/error/service_unavailable in shared controllers)
Go build: go build ./... in services/dsh/backend exit 0
design_debt: RESOLVED — DSH-001 control-panel layout and typography compositions moved to shared/app-shell; code guard passes.

donor_reference_policy.accepted_value: Workflow, information architecture, state vocabulary, and role-boundary reference
donor_reference_policy.target_new_repo_mapping: Map only through this Topic's declared shared brain, contracts, and surfaces
donor_reference_policy.implementation_requirement: Reimplement through generated clients and shared controllers; prove via code guards and tests

app_client: dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/HomeScreen.tsx — DshHomeGetScreen: store feed, category carousel, promos, video reels, inline search, favorites, filter rail. Uses useHomeState, useHomeDerivedStores, useHomeFilterRail, useHomeVideoHandlers.
dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/StoreScreen.tsx — DshStoreGetScreen: single store with product listings, category filter, search, favorites. Uses useStoreState, useStoreDerivedItems.
control_panel: dsh/frontend/control-panel/stores/ — store governance table, admin management (equivalent to new repo StoreManagementScreen)
app_partner: dsh/frontend/app-partner/ — partner own-store readiness reference; target implementation must add bounded own-store actions
app_field: dsh/frontend/app-field/ — field verification reference; target implementation must add assigned-store verification submission
app_captain: dsh/frontend/app-captain/ — pickup context reference; target implementation must add assigned pickup-point readiness reporting

donor_mappings.accepted_value: Store discovery information architecture, role-specific store context, and governance concepts
donor_mappings.target_new_repo_mapping: services/dsh/frontend/shared/store plus the five declared DSH surfaces
donor_mappings.implementation_requirement: Reimplement through generated clients and shared controllers; prove via code guards, tests, typecheck, and API smoke.

acceptance_criteria: Client sees store list with store name, category, open/closed status, and ETA in RTL layout
Client sees loading skeleton during store list fetch
Client sees empty state with descriptive message when no stores available in zone
Client sees error state with retry action when the response contract is invalid or a non-availability failure occurs
Client sees service-unavailable state with retry action when DSH cannot be reached
Client can filter stores by category, favorites, or nearest
Operator sees all stores in paginated admin table with search and status filters
Operator can open store detail panel with full store information (name, address, status, category)
Operator sees KPI strip (total stores, active, inactive counts)
Operator sees error/service-unavailable state when admin API fails
Partner can update own-store operating status and approved store settings with audited authorization
Field agent can submit assigned-store verification outcomes with evidence status
Captain can report assigned pickup-point readiness without changing order or delivery state
All surfaces enforce RTL layout with Arabic typography
RBAC: client sees only publicly visible stores; partner sees only own store; captain/field see only assigned store context; operator sees all stores
DSH-001.feature_behavior: Client opens app → store discovery list loads with active stores in zone → client filters by category → selects store → store detail page; Operator opens partners/stores → admin table with filters → opens detail panel → reviews store status

DSH-002.purpose: Personalized home screen with banners, promos, categories, and store feed for app-client. Home Discovery Admin for operators in control-panel.
DSH-002.business_meaning: The home screen is the primary customer entry point for commerce discovery. Operators must be able to manage what appears: banners for promotions, promos for discounts, category shortcuts for navigation. Bad home content = bad conversion.
in_scope: HomeDiscoveryScreen + HomeHeroBannerSection + HomePromoSection + HomeCategorySection + HomeFilterRailSection + HomeStoreFeedSection (app-client)
Home Discovery Admin control-panel screens: banners admin, promos admin, categories admin

real_experience_required.app_client: Customer sees home screen with banners, promos, category rail, store feed. Loading skeleton, empty states (no banners), error (API down), offline state screenshots required.
real_experience_required.control_panel_banners_admin: Operator can view banner list, create/edit/delete banner, see empty state (no banners), error state. Screenshots for all states.
real_experience_required.control_panel_promos_admin: Operator manages promotions list, create/edit/delete, empty, error states. Screenshots required.
real_experience_required.control_panel_categories_admin: Operator manages category list, reorder, empty, error states. Screenshots required.

partial_detail.app_client: RUNTIME_VERIFIED — HomeDiscoveryScreen and all home sections
partial_detail.control_panel: RUNTIME_VERIFIED — banners/promos/categories CRUD, audit, routes, and required visual states implemented

app_client: dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/HomeScreen.tsx — DshHomeGetScreen: full home layout with hero banner carousel, promo rail, category shortcuts, filter rail (all/nearest/offers/favorites/new), store feed, video reels. States: ready (full render), loading (skeleton while fetching). Uses useHomeState, useHomeDerivedStores, useHomePromoHandlers, useHomeFilterRail, useHomeVideoHandlers, useHomeTickerState.
control_panel: dsh/frontend/control-panel/marketing/ — marketing hub containing home discovery banner/promo/category admin (not fully detailed in donor analysis; expected under marketing section)

acceptance_criteria: Client home screen renders hero banner carousel (loading skeleton → banners visible)
Client home screen renders promo rail with active promotions
Client home screen renders category shortcuts for navigation
Client home screen renders filter rail: all / nearest / offers / favorites / new
Client home screen renders store feed with filtered results per active filter
Client sees empty state per section when no banners/promos configured for zone
Client sees error state with retry when home discovery API fails
Client sees offline state with cached last-known content or clear offline indicator
Operator can create, edit, delete banners for home screen (control-panel marketing)
Operator can manage promos: create, target by zone, set start/end date
Operator can manage category shortcuts: add, reorder, deactivate
All home content respects zone/city targeting — wrong-zone content never shown
All home sections render RTL with localized text and formatted dates/numbers
DSH-002.feature_behavior: Client opens app → home discovery API called with cityCode + serviceAreaCode → banners, promos, categories, stores returned → sections rendered with loading skeletons → filter rail defaults to 'all' → client taps filter → store feed updates with filtered results

DSH-003.purpose: Product catalog browse for customers, catalog management for partners, catalog governance for operators.
DSH-003.business_meaning: Without a catalog, no products can be ordered. Partners need to manage their menus and inventory. Customers browse and select products. Operators govern catalog quality, pricing compliance, and availability.
in_scope: Catalog API operations (list, get, create, update, archive)

real_experience_required.app_client: Customer browses catalog, sees products, loading skeleton, empty (no products), error states.
real_experience_required.app_partner: Partner manages catalog, adds/edits products, sees empty catalog, error, permission denied if unauthorized.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator views catalog governance table, product detail, compliance review, empty, error states.

app_client: dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/StoreItemsScreen.tsx — product listing screen for a single store (browse by category, search, add to cart)

acceptance_criteria: Client can browse product catalog for a store filtered by category
Client sees loading skeleton while catalog loads
Client sees empty state when no products available in selected category
Client sees error state with retry when catalog API fails
Partner can add a new product with name, description, price, category, media
Partner can edit an existing product's details
Partner can archive a product (no longer visible to clients)
Partner sees their own catalog only — cannot access other partner catalogs
Operator sees all products in governance table with search and status filters
Operator can review product in detail drawer with full audit trail
Operator can approve or reject product for publication
Operator can perform bulk archive or bulk activate operations
Operator can manage taxonomy tree for category assignment
Product publication check validates media, pricing, taxonomy before going live
All product names and descriptions support Arabic (RTL) content
RBAC: partner sees only own store catalog; operator sees all
DSH-003.feature_behavior: Partner adds product → product enters draft state → publication readiness check (media + pricing + taxonomy) → partner submits for review → appears in operator approval queue → operator reviews in detail drawer → approves → product becomes visible to clients in store catalog
evidence_required: Runtime API and database proof for the approved Topic scope
Per-surface visual evidence for every declared state

DSH-004.purpose: Client cart management with zone serviceability check. Customer selects products, verifies delivery zone, and gets order quote before checkout.
DSH-004.business_meaning: Cart is the bridge between discovery and purchase. Serviceability ensures delivery is possible to the customer's location. Without this, checkout cannot begin.

real_experience_required.app_client: Customer adds products, views cart, checks serviceability (success + out-of-zone blocked), sees error/empty states.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator sees cart activity summary, can identify stuck carts.

app_client: dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/CartScreen.tsx — DshCartUnifiedScreen (2419 lines): full cart editor with item list, qty controls, pricing summary (subtotal/delivery/discount/grand total in minor units), payment method selection (COD/wallet/mixed/official-wallets), fulfillment mode picker (bthwani_delivery/partner_delivery/pickup), delivery address, order notes, extra request, scheduling, product recommendations preview.
States: cart_empty, cart_ready, order_created, order_confirmed, payment_pending, wallet_credit_visible.

Financial boundary in donor: CartScreen imports useWltDshWalletSession for wallet balance display (read-only reference). Actual ledger mutation delegated to WLT. EXPERIMENTAL_PAYMENT_ENABLED=false flag controls live payment. All pricing shown as preview labels — no DSH-side amount calculation.

acceptance_criteria: Client can add items to cart from product catalog
Client sees cart with itemized list, quantities, and per-item pricing (from WLT reference)
Client can update quantities (increase/decrease) — cart total recalculated by WLT
Client sees empty cart state with CTA to browse stores
Client can select fulfillment mode: bthwani_delivery / partner_delivery / pickup
Client can select payment method: COD / wallet / official wallet — COD availability determined by zone and order value (WLT)
Client sees wallet balance display (read-only reference from WLT — no DSH-side wallet balance computation)
Client sees serviceability check result (in-zone → proceed; out-of-zone → blocked state with change address CTA)
Client blocked from proceeding if zone is inactive (out-of-zone serviceability state)
Client sees error state with retry when cart API fails
Client sees loading state during serviceability check
All pricing labels are display-only references — DSH never computes amounts
Fulfillment mode selection affects which delivery fee estimate is shown (from WLT reference)
DSH-004.feature_behavior: Client selects products → opens cart → reviews items + totals → selects payment method → selects fulfillment mode → serviceability check fires (DSH checks zone) → if in-zone: proceed button enabled → tap proceed → navigate to checkout intent (DSH-005)

DSH-005.purpose: Checkout intent shell on DSH side. Customer confirms order intent, DSH hands off to WLT payment session, WLT returns payment reference.
DSH-005.business_meaning: The checkout moment is the most critical customer journey point. DSH owns the intent and order creation. WLT owns the actual payment execution. The handoff contract between them must be clean and explicit.

real_experience_required.app_client: Customer reaches checkout, sees intent confirmation, WLT handoff reference, success and error paths.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator sees checkout intent activity with payment reference status.

DSH-005.wlt_dependency: WLT-001 minimal payment-session reference create/read is active. Payment execution, capture, refunds, settlement, wallet balance mutation, and ledger posting remain out of scope.
DSH-005.wlt_boundary_note: wlt_payment_session_id stores an opaque WLT-owned payment-session reference created by services/wlt. DSH never executes payment, refund, settlement, wallet mutation, ledger posting, provider capture, or financial truth mutation. guard:no-financial-mutation-outside-wlt: PASS.
DSH-005.slice_gate: Runtime smoke PASS on 2026-06-24 for WLT payment-session create/read and DSH checkout intent creation with non-empty wltPaymentSessionId.

app_client: States explicitly handled: ready (address + payment method + order summary + confirm button), loading ('Checking availability...'), quote-loading ('Calculating delivery cost...'), order-created (success with 'Track order' CTA), error/payment-failed ('Payment failed' + retry), quote-failed (delivery calculation error + retry), quote-success (delivery confirmed, ready to confirm), blocked/quote-unavailable ('Outside coverage area' + change address CTA).
CheckoutContextNotice components contain explicit WLT boundary documentation: 'Checkout intent يسلّم قرار الدفع إلى WLT فقط'

acceptance_criteria: Client sees checkout screen with address, payment method, delivery estimate, and order summary
Loading state shows 'Checking availability...' during serviceability re-check
Quote-loading state shows 'Calculating delivery cost...' during WLT quote fetch
Quote-success state shows confirmed delivery fee before client confirms
Quote-failed state shows error with retry CTA — DSH does not guess delivery fee
Blocked/quote-unavailable state shows 'Outside coverage area' with 'Change address' CTA
Order-created success state shows order reference with 'Track order' navigation CTA
Payment-failed state shows clear failure message with retry option
Client can navigate back from error/failed states without losing cart
DSH creates checkout intent with payment session reference from WLT — no financial mutation in DSH
Timeout state shown if WLT handoff exceeds configurable window
All 8 states have distinct UI and correct RTL layout
DSH-005.feature_behavior: Client taps 'Confirm order' in cart → checkout intent created in DSH → DSH calls WLT payment-sessions reference for payment authorization → WLT returns payment session ID → DSH receives session reference (not financial state) → quote confirmed → client taps 'Place order' → order created → order-created state shown → track order

DSH-006.purpose: Order lifecycle from creation to partner acceptance. Customer places order, partner receives and accepts/declines.
DSH-006.business_meaning: Order creation is when commerce intent becomes a business transaction. Partners must be able to accept orders in real-time. Operators monitor order queue health.

real_experience_required.app_client: Customer sees order list, order detail, all order status transitions.
real_experience_required.app_partner: Partner sees incoming order, accepts, declines, sees empty inbox.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator sees live order queue with all status states.

app_client: dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/DshOrdersListScreen.tsx — DshOrdersListScreen: order history list with search, all order status labels
dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/parts/OrdersTrackingHelpers.tsx — CreateOrderJourneyScreen: order timeline rendering with all journey steps
app_partner: dsh/frontend/app-partner/screens/OrdersInboxScreen.tsx — PartnerOrdersHomeScreen: stage filters (all, acceptance, preparation, ready, handoff, delivering, issues). Quick filters: urgent, sla_risk, unread, pickup, partner_delivery, bthwani_delivery, completed, cancelled. Sort: next_action, newest, priority, sla. States: ready | loading | empty | error | offline | disabled | partial.
control_panel: dsh/frontend/control-panel/operations/ — order queue view with fulfillment mode filters

acceptance_criteria: Client sees order list with status labels for all 15 order states
Client sees order detail with timeline showing journey steps
Client can cancel order when cancellable (CancelOrderSheet appears)
Client sees loading, empty (no orders), error states
Partner order inbox shows stage filters: acceptance, preparation, ready, handoff, delivering, issues
Partner can filter by: urgent, sla_risk, unread, pickup mode, delivery mode
Partner can sort by: next_action, newest, priority, sla
Partner sees loading, empty inbox, error, offline, disabled states
Partner can accept order within acceptance window
Partner can reject order with mandatory reason selection
Operator sees live order queue in control-panel operations with all status states
Order status transitions follow DSH order state machine: pending → store-accepted → preparing → ready-for-pickup → dispatching...
All state labels are localized (RTL + Arabic)
DSH-006.feature_behavior: Client places order → order created in DSH with state=pending → partner inbox gets order notification → partner opens inbox → stage filter shows new order in 'acceptance' stage → partner taps accept → state transitions to store-accepted → preparing → marks ready → state=ready-for-pickup → dispatch starts

DSH-007.purpose: Order assignment to captain, pickup, delivery, proof of delivery submission.
DSH-007.business_meaning: Last-mile delivery is the final touchpoint with the customer. Dispatch determines which captain handles which order. The delivery lifecycle — from pickup to PoD — must be traceable by customer and operator.

real_experience_required.app_captain: Captain sees order queue, accepts pickup, navigates to store, confirms pickup, delivers, submits PoD.
real_experience_required.app_client: Customer sees live tracking from pickup to delivery.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator sees dispatch room with active assignments, live order positions.

verified_runtime_summary.api_smoke: PASS — local DSH API on 58081 with runtime Postgres/Identity: operator assignment, captain accept/status transitions, client tracking, PoD delivered
verified_runtime_summary.migration: PASS — dsh-007_dispatch.sql applied by pnpm run runtime:migrate
verified_runtime_summary.backend: PASS — go test ./... and go build ./...

app_captain: dsh/frontend/app-captain/screens/DshCaptainOrdersScreen.tsx — sections: inbox, detail, chat, bell, action, proof, support. States: availability-toggle, loading-assignment, and all DshCaptainOrdersScreenState variants.
dsh/frontend/app-captain/screens/DshCaptainMapScreen.tsx — map-based delivery view with captain position and destination
dsh/frontend/app-captain/screens/DshCaptainPickupDropoffScreen.tsx — pickup confirmation and dropoff confirmation flow
dsh/frontend/app-captain/screens/DshCaptainPoDSubmissionScreen.tsx — Proof of Delivery submission (photo + signature or code)
dsh/frontend/app-captain/screens/DshCaptainStoreCourierHomeContent.tsx — home content for store courier mode
app_client: dsh/frontend/app-client/screens/DshTrackingScreen.tsx — live tracking view with all order lifecycle states, captain position, ETA
control_panel: dsh/frontend/control-panel/operations/DispatchAssignmentScreen.tsx — manual captain assignment from operations room

acceptance_criteria: Captain can toggle availability (online/offline); only online captains receive dispatch assignments
Captain receives task assignment; sees order detail with store address and delivery address
Captain can accept or decline assignment within configurable time window
Auto-reject fires if captain does not respond within time window
Captain navigates to store on map; confirms arrival at store
Captain confirms pickup from partner (order state: picked-up)
Captain navigates to customer on map; confirms arrival at customer
Captain submits Proof of Delivery (photo or confirmation code)
PoD submission transitions order state to delivered
Captain COD reconciliation view is read-only (WLT reference — no financial mutation in DSH)
Client sees live tracking screen with captain position and ETA from pickup
Client sees delivered state after PoD submission
Operator can manually assign captain from dispatch room if auto-dispatch fails
All captain screens work in RTL layout
RBAC: captain sees only their own assigned orders
DSH-007.feature_behavior: Order state=ready-for-pickup → dispatch engine runs radial search → finds online captain within radius → sends assignment offer → captain accepts → state=driver-assigned → captain navigates to store → confirms arrived (state=driver-arrived-store) → picks up order (state=picked-up) → navigates to customer → confirms arrived (state=arrived-customer) → submits PoD → state=delivered

DSH-008.purpose: Field agent store onboarding, visit recording, readiness escalation, document upload. Partners see their onboarding status.
DSH-008.business_meaning: Field agents are the quality gatekeepers for the store network. Stores must be physically verified before going live. Partners need to know their onboarding progress. Operators govern the readiness pipeline.
in_scope: Field readiness queue in control-panel partners section

real_experience_required.app_field: Field agent creates visit, completes readiness checklist, uploads documents, escalates if issues found.
real_experience_required.app_partner: Partner sees onboarding status, pending items, completion confirmation.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator sees readiness queue, approves/rejects submissions, escalation inbox.

app_field: dsh/frontend/app-field/screens/DshFieldStoresScreen.tsx — stores list with filters: today, urgent, completed, all. States: ready | loading | empty | error | offline.
dsh/frontend/app-field/screens/DshFieldProfileHomeScreen.tsx — field agent home with assignment summary
app_partner: dsh/frontend/app-partner/screens/StoreProfileScreen.tsx — partner sees own onboarding status and readiness progress
control_panel: dsh/frontend/control-panel/partners/ — field readiness queue, document review workspace, partner performance screen

shared_brain_contracts.binding_contracts: DSH_FIELD_BINDING_CONTRACTS — contract gating for field assignment

acceptance_criteria: Field agent home shows assigned stores with filter: today's visits, urgent, completed, all
Field agent sees loading, empty (no assignments), error, offline states
Field agent opens store visit → records visit with timestamped checklist
Field agent completes store onboarding steps in sequence — cannot skip required steps
Field agent uploads documents (license, photos) — upload progress shown
Field agent can escalate readiness issues from escalation screen with mandatory description
Partner sees their own onboarding status and progress percentage
Partner sees pending items blocking activation
Operator sees field readiness queue with pending store visits
Operator can review submitted documents
Operator can approve or escalate further
Readiness escalation triggers admin notification (via DSH-011)
Field agent sees offline state with queued actions when connection lost
RTL layout and Arabic labels throughout
RBAC: field agent sees only their assigned stores; partner sees only own store onboarding
DSH-008.feature_behavior: Operator assigns field agent to store → agent receives task notification (DSH-011) → agent opens DshFieldStoresScreen → filters by today → opens store → completes visit checklist step by step → uploads documents → if issues: escalates from DshFieldReadinessEscalationScreen → operator notified → operator reviews in readiness queue → approves → partner notified → onboarding status updated
DSH-008.note: DSH-001 app-field context layer is done (store verification context). Full field onboarding workflow is this Topic.

DSH-009.purpose: Support ticket queue, case management, actor-facing ticket submission, operations intervention room.
DSH-009.business_meaning: Every platform needs a support feedback loop. Customers escalate order issues. Partners report operational problems. Captains flag delivery complications. Operators manage resolution. Without this, trust erodes.

real_experience_required.app_client: Customer creates ticket, sees open/resolved states.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator sees support queue, case detail, intervention room with all states.

DSH-010.purpose: Read-only finance visibility for DSH operations. Displays WLT-provided settlement and payment status summaries. No financial mutation within DSH.
DSH-010.business_meaning: Operators need financial visibility to manage the platform, detect settlement issues, and respond to partner queries. This is display-only — DSH shows what WLT owns. The boundary is absolute.
in_scope: No new DSH tables — reads from WLT reference tables only

real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator sees settlement summary, payment status overview. WLT unavailable state must be shown. ALL states with screenshots required.
real_experience_required.app_partner_read_only: Partner sees their settlement status display. Loading, success, error states required.

DSH-010.constraint: READ-ONLY. No financial mutation. DSH may only display WLT-provided statuses.
DSH-010.wlt_dependency: WLT settlement-summary read endpoint (requires WLT-001 approval)

control_panel: WltFinanceReadOnlySkeletonProof.tsx (skeleton proof only — not production screen)
app_partner: Partner finance/settlement status display (donor: DshPartnerFinanceScreen — read-only WLT reference)

acceptance_criteria: Operator sees settlement summary table sourced from WLT — read-only, no edit/delete actions visible
Operator sees payment status overview — no mutation buttons anywhere in this section
WLT unavailable state shown with clear message when WLT API is down
Partner sees their settlement status display — read-only
Loading skeleton shown while WLT data fetches
Error state shown with retry when WLT reference endpoint fails
FinanceReadOnlyFrame archetype enforced — no custom finance layout permitted
All amounts displayed in localized format (RTL, Arabic typography)
Guard: no DSH-side financial calculation or mutation code present
DSH-010.feature_behavior: Operator opens finance section → DSH reads WLT settlement-summary reference endpoint → displays partner settlement statuses and payment summaries in FinanceReadOnlyFrame → no interactive mutations possible — display only

DSH-011.purpose: Push notifications, in-app notification feed, OTP delivery, and system messages across all actor surfaces. Notification configuration and audit in control-panel platform section.
DSH-011.business_meaning: Real-time communication is critical to order lifecycle execution. Customers must know when their order is accepted, dispatched, and delivered. Partners must react to incoming orders immediately. Captains need task assignments within seconds. Delayed or missed notifications directly cause order failures and partner SLA breaches.
in_scope: Notification API operations: listDshNotifications, markDshNotificationRead, updateDshNotificationPreferences
out_of_scope: SMS/email delivery infrastructure (external provider — decided at DSH-011 approval)
Push notification provider SDK (infrastructure decision — abstract contract only)
WLT financial notification content (WLT owns wlt.financial.* event routing)

real_experience_required.app_client: Client sees bell icon with unread count badge; opens notification center (loading skeleton → list → empty when none); marks individual or all as read; sees notification detail.
real_experience_required.app_partner: Partner receives order notification; sees unread badge on inbox; opens notification with order summary.
real_experience_required.app_captain: Captain receives task assignment alert with order details; dismisses; sees empty when no tasks.
real_experience_required.app_field: Field agent receives store visit assignment; sees loading, notification detail, empty states.
real_experience_required.control_panel: Operator configures notification rules per event type per actor; views notification audit trail filtered by actor/event/date.

control_panel: Notification configuration — platform section; no dedicated screen found in donor; expected in platform section

acceptance_criteria: Client bell icon shows accurate unread count badge updated in real-time
Client notification center shows loading skeleton during fetch
Client notification center shows empty state with descriptive message when no notifications
Client can mark individual notification as read; badge count decrements
Client can mark all notifications as read; badge clears
Partner receives in-app notification when new order arrives; unread badge appears on inbox
Captain receives task assignment notification within configurable SLA window (default: 5s)
Field agent receives store visit assignment notification
Operator can view notification audit trail filtered by actor_type, event_type, and date range
Operator can configure which notification types are sent per actor type
Notification content uses translation keys only — no hardcoded strings
RTL layout enforced across all notification surfaces
RBAC: each actor sees only their own notifications; operator sees audit of all
DSH-011.feature_behavior: Order state transition fires → DSH notification backend emits typed event with correlation_id → notification service picks up event → sends push + in-app notification to actor(s) per trigger rule → actor surface shows unread badge → actor opens notification → marked as read with audit entry
tests_required: Event-to-actor routing rule tests (each trigger fires correct actor set)

DSH-012.purpose: Full marketing operations hub in control-panel: campaign management, loyalty programs, partner offers approval, video content governance, smart signal marketing, and extended banner/promo operations beyond home discovery.
DSH-012.business_meaning: Marketing drives discovery conversion and repeat orders. Operators run campaigns targeting zones and customer segments. Partners submit promotional offers for approval. Loyalty programs retain customers. Smart signals enable algorithmic content placement. Without this, the platform has no growth lever beyond organic discovery.
in_scope: dsh_campaigns, dsh_loyalty_config, dsh_partner_offers, dsh_video_assets, dsh_smart_signals tables
Marketing API operations: campaigns CRUD, loyalty config, partner offer approval workflow, video assets, smart signals
BannersCommandDeckScreen — full banner lifecycle (draft → scheduled → live → expired)
PromosCommandDeckScreen — promo management with zone and segment targeting
CampaignsCommandDeckScreen — campaign manager with scheduling and targeting
ControlPanelDshMarketingApprovalScreen — approval queue for partner-submitted promotions
VideosCommandDeckScreen — video content governance and review queue
out_of_scope: Home discovery basic banners/promos/categories (DSH-002 — basic home content management)
Financial discount application at checkout (WLT — promo codes applied by WLT at payment)
Customer analytics and segmentation data (future analytics topic)

real_experience_required.control_panel_campaigns: Operator creates campaign with zone, date range, targeting. Views active/paused/completed with counts. Empty state when no campaigns. Error state.
real_experience_required.control_panel_banners: Operator manages full banner lifecycle: create, schedule publish date, publish, expire. All status states visible.
real_experience_required.control_panel_partner_offers: Operator reviews partner-submitted promos in approval queue. Approves or rejects with mandatory reason. Rejected offer returns to partner.
real_experience_required.control_panel_loyalty: Operator configures loyalty reward rules per tier. Updates config. Sees validation errors.
real_experience_required.control_panel_smart_signals: Operator configures algorithmic placement weights. Reviews live signal distribution.

acceptance_criteria: Operator can create campaign with zone targeting, start/end date, and content reference
Campaign status transitions: scheduled → active (on start date) → completed (on end date) → archived
Operator can pause/resume active campaign
Empty state shown with CTA when no campaigns exist
Error state with retry when API fails
Partner-submitted offers appear in approval queue with submission timestamp
Operator can approve offer; it becomes live and visible to customers in zone
Operator can reject offer; rejection reason is mandatory; partner notified via DSH-011
Loyalty reward rules are configurable per tier with input validation
Video assets can be reviewed and approved/rejected for use in home feed
Smart signal weights are configurable; changes take effect within next discovery refresh cycle
All marketing content respects zone/city targeting — wrong-zone content never surfaces to customers
Approval actions are audited with actor_id, action, reason, timestamp
RTL layout and Arabic content support throughout
DSH-012.feature_behavior: Operator opens marketing hub → navigates to campaigns → creates campaign (zone + schedule + content) → campaign goes live on start date → appears in customer home via DSH-002 discovery layer; separately: partner submits promotional offer → appears in approval queue → operator reviews and approves → offer active in customer zone

DSH-013.purpose: Service area polygon management, zone configuration, SLA rules, delivery time windows, capacity planning, and operational policies that govern the entire platform's geographic and performance boundaries.
DSH-013.business_meaning: Platform policies define where the service operates, at what speed, and under what performance standards. Zone configurations affect serviceability checks for every customer's checkout. SLA rules govern captain and partner performance. Incorrect zone data causes customer frustration at checkout when valid addresses appear unserviced.
in_scope: Platform policy API operations: zone CRUD, SLA rules, capacity config
ServiceAreaManagementScreen (control-panel platform section — map + zone list)
ZoneConfigurationScreen (zone rules, delivery windows, polygon editor)
SLARulesConfigScreen (captain SLA, partner SLA, configuration versioning)
CapacityPlanningScreen (max concurrent orders per zone, threshold alerts)
GeoHeatmapScreen (real-time demand view — read-only, linked from operations)
out_of_scope: Dispatch assignment execution logic (DSH-007 — uses zone config but owns dispatch)
Financial pricing rules and delivery fee calculation (WLT — consumes zone data for pricing)
Captain GPS tracking infrastructure (external maps provider)

real_experience_required.control_panel_service_areas: Operator views all service zones on a map with active/inactive status. Can activate or deactivate zones with confirmation prompt. Empty state when no zones defined. Error state.
real_experience_required.control_panel_sla: Operator views and edits SLA rules per zone and order category. Changes are versioned and audited. Validation error shown for invalid values.
real_experience_required.control_panel_capacity: Operator configures maximum concurrent orders per zone. Threshold warning configurable.
real_experience_required.control_panel_heatmap: Real-time demand heatmap overlay; read-only; sourced from live order data.

control_panel: dsh/frontend/control-panel/operations/ — area capacity screen (linked from operations room)
dsh/frontend/control-panel/operations/ — geo heatmap view (accessed from operations context)

acceptance_criteria: Operator can view all service zones on map with status badges (active/inactive)
Operator can activate a zone; confirmation required; change is audited
Operator can deactivate a zone; confirmation required; serviceability check for that zone immediately returns 'out of zone'
Zone deactivation propagates to DSH-005 serviceability check within one request cycle
SLA rules can be configured per zone with numeric validation
SLA configuration changes are versioned — previous version preserved in audit
Capacity thresholds configurable per zone with warning level and hard limit
Geo heatmap shows real-time demand — read-only, no edits on heatmap view
Empty state when no zones are configured
Error state with retry when API fails
RTL layout and Arabic labels required throughout
All zone changes logged with actor_id, action, zone_id, from_state, to_state, timestamp
DSH-013.feature_behavior: Operator opens platform section → views service area map → selects zone → deactivates zone → system updates zone_config → next DSH-005 serviceability check for customer address in that zone returns 'out of zone' → customer sees 'Outside coverage area' state in checkout
tests_required: Serviceability propagation test (deactivated zone blocks checkout)
SLA rule validation and versioning tests

DSH-014.purpose: Staff role management, permission assignment, approval workflows, access governance for operators. Partner activation governance, document review, and onboarding approval workflows.
DSH-014.business_meaning: Role-based access control must be governed by a human admin. New operators must be provisioned with appropriate scopes. Partner activations require physical document verification and ops approval. Captain and field agent onboarding requires credentialing review. Without this topic, the platform cannot safely manage its internal staff or grow its partner network.
in_scope: Role and staff management backend handlers
Administration API operations: role CRUD, staff assignment, partner activation workflow
AuditTrailAdminScreen (full platform audit with actor/date/action filters)
out_of_scope: Customer account management (out of current scope)
Financial role assignments (WLT — WLT owns financial actor permissions)
HR management (out of current scope for current slices)
Field agent store assignment routing (DSH-008 — field readiness topic)
Marketing approval workflow (DSH-012 — owns partner offer approval)

real_experience_required.control_panel_roles: Super-admin views staff role list. Creates role with permission scope. Assigns role to operator. Empty state. Error state.
real_experience_required.control_panel_partner_activation: Admin reviews partner onboarding queue. Opens document review workspace. Approves → partner becomes active. Rejects → mandatory reason; partner notified. Blocks partner → all surfaces deny access.
real_experience_required.control_panel_audit: Admin views complete platform audit trail. Filters by actor_type, action, date range. Exports audit subset.

partner_activation_states.transition_rules: submitted → ops_approved (document check) → partner_active (activation confirmed) → client_visible (public listing); blocked at any point suspends all partner access

control_panel: dsh/frontend/control-panel/partners/PartnerActivationWorkspace.tsx — ControlPanelDshPartnerActivationScreen: manages DshPartnerActivationStatus (client_visible, partner_active, ops_approved, blocked) with marketing eligibility checks
dsh/frontend/control-panel/partners/ — partner deactivation workspace, document review workspace, complaints handling, performance review, catalog override workspaces
dsh/frontend/control-panel/support/AuditTrailDetailPanel.tsx — audit trail detail view (reused in administration section)

acceptance_criteria: Super-admin can create staff role with named permission scopes
Super-admin can assign role to operator; operator receives that scope on next login
Role assignment and revocation are audited with actor_id, target_actor_id, role, action, timestamp
Partner activation queue shows submitted partners with submission date
Admin can open document review workspace showing uploaded partner documents
Admin can approve partner activation; partner status transitions to partner_active → client_visible
Admin can reject partner activation; rejection reason is mandatory; partner receives notification via DSH-011
Admin can block partner at any activation stage; all partner surfaces immediately deny access
Audit trail is filterable by actor_type, action type, date range
Audit trail shows all admin actions including role assignments, partner activations, rejections
Empty state shown when no pending approvals
Error state with retry when API fails
RTL layout and Arabic content support throughout
DSH-014.feature_behavior: Partner submits onboarding documents via DSH-008 → partner activation request appears in admin queue → admin opens document review workspace → reviews documents → approves (partner status: ops_approved → partner_active → client_visible) or rejects with reason → partner receives notification (DSH-011) → all partner surfaces reflect updated activation status
tests_required: Partner activation state machine transition tests

WLT-001.purpose: Full payment session lifecycle: authorize, capture, COD collection, expiry. WLT owns all financial state transitions.
WLT-001.business_meaning: Payment capture is the moment financial truth is established. WLT must own authorize→capture→settle transitions exclusively. DSH only reads the opaque payment session reference ID.
in_scope: Payment session status expansion: authorized, captured, cod_pending, cod_collected
AuthorizeSession, CaptureSession, MarkCodPending, MarkCodCollected, ExpireSession Go domain
OpenAPI operations: authorizeWltPaymentSession, captureWltPaymentSession, expireWltPaymentSession, markWltCodCollected
WltPaymentSession extended with amount_minor_units, currency, captured_at
out_of_scope: Provider payment gateway integration (external — future topic)
WLT-001.approval_note: User approved 2026-06-24 — financial mutation APIs unlocked

WLT-002.purpose: Full refund lifecycle owned by WLT: request, approve, process, complete, reject. DSH only reads refund status reference.
WLT-002.business_meaning: Refunds are a financial truth mutation — only WLT may initiate, process, and complete them. DSH surfaces display the refund status as a read-only reference.
in_scope: CreateRefund, GetRefund, ListRefunds, ApproveRefund, CompleteRefund, RejectRefund Go domain
HTTP routes: POST /wlt/refunds, GET /wlt/refunds/{id}, GET /wlt/refunds, POST approve|complete|reject
OpenAPI: createWltRefund, getWltRefund, listWltRefunds, approveWltRefund, completeWltRefund, rejectWltRefund

WLT-003.purpose: Partner settlement lifecycle: create, post, list, summarize. WLT owns all settlement state. DSH-010 finance section reads settlement summaries as display-only.
WLT-003.business_meaning: Settlement determines when partners get paid. WLT must own the settlement ledger exclusively. DSH-010 may display settlement status summaries to operators — but cannot mutate them.
in_scope: CreateSettlement, GetSettlement, ListPartnerSettlements, ListSettlementSummary, PostSettlement Go domain
OpenAPI: createWltSettlement, getWltSettlement, listWltSettlements, postWltSettlement, getWltSettlementSummary
read_only_dsh_surfaces: control-panel (finance section — display only via DSH-010)

WLT-004.purpose: COD collection tracking and platform commission records. WLT owns financial COD state. Captain app reads COD status as display-only reference.
WLT-004.business_meaning: COD (Cash On Delivery) creates a financial obligation for captains to remit collected cash. Platform commissions (delivery fee, COD fee) must be tracked. WLT owns both. DSH captain surface shows read-only COD status.
in_scope: CreateCodRecord, GetCodRecord, ListCodRecords, MarkCodCollected, MarkCodRemitted, CreateCommission, ListCommissions Go domain
HTTP routes: POST|GET /wlt/cod-records, POST collect|remit, POST|GET /wlt/commissions
OpenAPI: createWltCodRecord, getWltCodRecord, listWltCodRecords, collectWltCod, remitWltCod, createWltCommission, listWltCommissions
DSH bridge: services/dsh/frontend/shared/wlt-cod/ (captain COD finance display)

WLT-005.purpose: Append-only financial ledger audit trail. WLT posts entries for every financial state transition. Read-only for admin audit.
WLT-005.business_meaning: Every financial event must leave an immutable audit trail. WLT posts ledger entries for payment captures, refunds, settlements, and COD collections. Operators can query the ledger for dispute resolution and compliance.
in_scope: AppendLedgerEntry, GetLedgerEntry, ListLedgerEntries (cursor pagination) Go domain
DSH bridge: services/dsh/frontend/shared/wlt-ledger/ (admin audit read-only display)
